digest.greeting: Monday, July 6, Jean. Today has 6 visible calendar blocks, anchored by CoE Newsletter Development, BD Portfolio Review, and the Maria 1:1. GitHub state leaves 6 meaningful carried-over items and 2 active reminders from Friday, with Amazon Connect and dashboard publish confidence as the clearest urgent risks. Keep the day centered on closing the Amazon Connect delay response, scheduling portfolio-owner conversations, setting up the SMS marketing-number call, and using the portfolio/review blocks to clarify blockers and next steps. Direct Teams reads and the tomorrow calendar preview were rate-limited this morning, so Teams is used only where Outlook surfaced notification context.

items.label: Close the Amazon Connect delay response
items.detail: This remains the most decision-sensitive open item from Friday. Follow up with Tiago and confirm whether Danny and Jeff now have the summary they need on the Amazon Connect and Agentforce Voice delays.

items.label: Confirm the dashboard publish path is current
items.detail: Friday's inbox still showed pmdashboard deployment noise, including Vercel rate-limit failures and a later ready preview. After today's pending-digest workflow runs, confirm the GitHub Pages dashboard starts with today's digest and that the queue file was consumed.

items.label: Schedule the portfolio-owner meetings
items.detail: Use the lighter morning or the noon protected block to get the portfolio-owner conversations on the calendar before the week fills in around BD Portfolio Review and 1:1 follow-ups.

items.label: Set up the SMS marketing-number call
items.detail: Deepak's Friday guidance said the team does not need to wait for Josh's July 9 return. Move the call with Anu and Tim onto the calendar so SMS Automation keeps momentum.

items.label: Confirm the executive project deck update path
items.detail: The Friday deck invite changed ownership and attendees. Confirm the update path still covers Amazon Connect, SMS Automation, and intake/process changes cleanly enough for leadership visibility this week.

items.label: Review Tomas Aparicio's SOP edit-access request
items.detail: The SharePoint request remains a permission decision. Approve only if Tomas should be able to edit, share, and download the SOP copy.

items.label: Schedule meetings with portfolio owners
items.detail: One-shot reminder still active in GitHub state. Treat this as the calendar action behind the carried-over portfolio-owner item, then clear it after today's digest publishes.

items.label: Follow up with Tiago on Amazon Connect
items.detail: One-shot reminder still active in GitHub state. Tie the follow-up directly to the open Amazon Connect delay-response work so the decision path does not drift into another day.

sections.title: Today's Schedule
sections.note: Times are shown in Jean's Outlook working timezone. The day has a clear prep window before 11:00 AM, a protected noon block, and a 4:00 PM focus window after the Maria 1:1.

items.label: Approve Timecards & Prepare for the Week
items.detail: Start the week by clearing timecards and using the early open space to triage Friday's carry-over work.

items.label: CoE Newsletter Development
items.detail: Jean and Mar are on the invite. Use this as the main CoE content-development block and keep any newsletter decisions explicit before the noon protected time.

items.label: Blocked Space
items.detail: Best protected window for Amazon Connect follow-up, portfolio-owner scheduling, and quick dashboard publish verification.

items.label: BD Portfolio Review
items.detail: Weekly portfolio touchpoint with Matthew. The meeting description calls out active project status, risks, blockers, and new intake, so bring only the items that need priority alignment or a next owner.

items.label: 1:1 Maria & Jean
items.detail: Agenda is portfolio review, roadblocks, and next steps. Use it to turn open Maria-linked items into clear owners rather than leaving them as general status.

items.label: Focus time
items.detail: Use this final protected hour to close any follow-up created by BD Portfolio Review or the Maria 1:1 while it is still fresh.

items.label: Prepare the CoE newsletter decisions
items.detail: Before the 11:00 AM working session with Mar, identify what content needs approval, what can be drafted now, and what should be deferred so the newsletter work does not stay open-ended.

items.label: Bring blockers and intake choices to BD Portfolio Review
items.detail: The BD Portfolio Review agenda is built around active project status, key risks or blockers, and new intake. Go in with the few items that need priority alignment, not a broad status pass.

items.label: Use the Maria 1:1 to assign next owners
items.detail: The 1:1 agenda calls for portfolio review, roadblocks, and next steps. Convert any unresolved project blockers into named follow-ups before the 4:00 PM focus block.

items.label: Review the Court Litify development path
items.detail: Jorge scheduled Court Litify Dev and separately wrote Anu, with Jean copied, about whether Court Litify improvements should go through CoE from an IT standpoint. Review whether this needs Jean input before that assessment becomes the recommendation path.
